key_departures.impact: Super Bowl LX MVP and Seattle's primary ball carrier for four seasons. Rushed for 3,555 yards and 29 TDs over career. Departed with Zach Charbonnet also recovering from a torn ACL suffered in the playoffs. Leaves a major void in an otherwise run-first offense.

Second-round pick in 2022, Mafe was a core piece of Seattle's EDGE rotation alongside DeMarcus Lawrence, Derick Hall, and Uchenna Nwosu. Recorded 20.0 sacks over four seasons. His departure thins an EDGE group that already had depth questions behind Lawrence (age/durability concerns) and Hall.

Started 26 of 32 games over the past two seasons at safety. Recorded 7 INTs, 13 PDs, 2 FFs, and 139 tackles. Was a versatile, ball-hawking presence alongside Julian Love. Ty Okada (ERFA, tendered) and Nick Emmanwori are in-house replacements, but both are unproven at full-time starter level.

Former Pro Bowler who had 12 INTs over four seasons but had lost his starting role to Josh Jobe by 2025. Market was softer than expected (1-year deal). Loss creates depth concern at CB2/CB3 behind Witherspoon and Jobe.
Followed former OC Klint Kubiak to Las Vegas. Primarily a special teams contributor and depth WR. Loss is manageable given the addition of Rashid Shaheed and depth at receiver.

signings.previous_team: Seattle Seahawks (re-sign; acquired via trade from Saints, Nov 2025)
signings.analysis: Retention of a key piece from the Super Bowl run. Shaheed was the only player in the NFL with both kick and punt return TDs in 2025, earning Pro Bowl honors as a returner. His re-signing at 3yr/$51M ($34.7M guaranteed) is a real investment — this is Seattle's guy at the WR3/returner role. WR need remains non-existent short-term with JSN, Kupp, Shaheed, and Horton. The concern is whether Seattle gets more offensive production from Shaheed to justify the AAV for a run-first team.

Critical re-signing. Jobe started 15 games in 2025 and posted the 4th-lowest completion percentage allowed in the NFL (47.7% on 50+ targets). 3yr/$24M at $8M AAV is outstanding value for a starting-caliber corner — significantly below the starter market tier. With Devon Witherspoon on his rookie deal, Seattle returns both starters at CB. However, Riq Woolen's departure means CB depth is thin, and the CB room now needs a reliable CB3. Jobe's retention prevents CB from being a top-tier need but does not eliminate it entirely.

Steal of the offseason. Thomas recorded 96 tackles, 10 TFLs, 3.5 sacks, 8 PDs, and 1 INT as a first-year starter in 2025. 2yr/$8M ($3M guaranteed) ranks 37th in LB AAV league-wide for a player who arguably should have been a Pro Bowler. With Thomas and Ernest Jones IV both under multi-year deals and Tyrice Knight also retained, LB is locked up as a non-need heading into the draft.

Low-cost depth signing after Walker's departure. Wilson rushed for 1,083 yards at 4.5 YPC over three seasons with Green Bay and had a 107-yard, 2-TD breakout in Week 12 2025. However, with Charbonnet (recovering from torn ACL, timeline uncertain), George Holani (ERFA), and this 1yr/$2.1M deal being described by Schneider himself as a 'come be part of our culture' invite, RB remains a significant draft and free agency need. One-year, near-minimum deal signals bridge, not solution.

First external signing of Seattle's 2026 free agency. Thomas is a Yale product who started 25 games over first two Colts seasons (6 INTs, 10 PDs) but has been primarily a special teams player and rotational safety since 2024. One-year deal at below-comp-pick-formula cost signals this is depth/competition, not a starter investment. Safety need remains: Ty Okada and Nick Emmanwori will battle to start alongside Julian Love, with Thomas adding experience and special teams value. S is still a draft-day consideration.

Re-signed safety/special teams contributor who blocked a punt vs. New Orleans and played 251 special teams snaps in 2025. One-year deal is clearly a special teams retention, not a solution to the Coby Bryant void. Adds competition and ST depth but does not move the needle on safety need.

Re-signed on undisclosed terms. Finley missed all of 2025 with a knee injury and projects as a practice squad/53-man roster bubble player. Pure depth — does not impact starting safety need assessment. Contract terms not yet disclosed; likely near-minimum.

Re-signed fullback/TE hybrid and special teams captain. Led all NFL players with 394 special teams snaps in 2025. Two-year deal in lieu of RFA tender. Critical to Seattle's elite special teams unit but not a factor in addressing any offensive need. Contract terms not yet disclosed.

Nose tackle re-signed on a 1yr/$2M deal. Rotational IDL/NT who provides interior depth alongside Leonard Williams. Below market for any real starter-tier IDL. IDL was not a pressing need entering FA given Williams is retained on a multi-year deal.

Re-signed swing tackle who stepped in admirably for injured Charles Cross in the final 3 regular season games (wins over LAR, CAR, SF). Plays both OT spots and provides crucial depth. 1-year, near-minimum deal means this is a depth retention, not a starter investment. OT was not a pre-FA need given Cross and Lucas are locked up, so this simply preserves continuity. Spotrac lists AAV at $1,085,000; very likely below the comp-pick formula threshold.

Former first-round pick (No. 30, MIA 2020) signing near the minimum. In 2024 with Washington he started 10 games and had a career-high 818 snaps when Forbes was injured. His 2025 role shrunk to 37% of snaps. Primarily a nickel corner, he gives Seattle a fifth CB behind Witherspoon, Jobe, Pritchett, and Jean-Charles. One-year/minimum deal means this is competition for depth snaps, not a starter investment. CB depth improves modestly, but need for a legitimate CB3 remains.
Re-signed after spending 2025 on the practice squad without playing a defensive snap. Cheap re-sign for depth. Does not address starter-quality CB need.

Note: Lock's 2-year/$5M deal was signed in April 2025 (Year 1 of the contract). He is already on the roster entering 2026 (Year 2 of deal at $2.75M cap hit). Listed here for completeness — the signing is not a 2026 FA move but was already reflected on the roster. Lock provides quality backup depth behind Sam Darnold. QB is not a need.
Note: Saubert was signed in March 2025 (appeared in all 17 games for SEA in 2025). Already on the roster. Listed for completeness. TE depth is solid with Noah Fant, AJ Barner, Russell, and Saubert.
Note: Cracraft was signed March 21, 2025. Already on the 2025 roster. WSU product provides receiver depth and return utility. Minimum deal. WR depth is not a concern.
Special teams specialist retained. Played 176 snaps exclusively on special teams in 2025. Does not impact LB depth chart need, which is already well-addressed.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Kenneth Walker III departed to Kansas City on a 3yr/$45M deal (highest-paid RB ever). Zach Charbonnet is recovering from a torn ACL suffered in the divisional round and his 2026 availability is uncertain — Seahawks HC said combine reports were 'positive' but timeline is likely to miss much of the season. Emanuel Wilson (1yr/$2.1M) is a bridge body, not a solution. George Holani (ERFA) is not an every-down back. Seattle is a run-first team with Sam Darnold at QB. RB is a top-3 draft priority.

Boye Mafe departed to Cincinnati on 3yr/$60M, removing a key pass rusher from a deep rotation. The group still includes DeMarcus Lawrence (age/durability concern at 33), Derick Hall, Uchenna Nwosu, and Jared Ivey. No meaningful external signing at EDGE. The rotation has bodies but no elite ascending pass rusher. Need stays at tier 2 — drafting a developmental EDGE in rounds 1-3 is a priority to replace Mafe's production and cover Lawrence's age risk.

Coby Bryant departed to Chicago on 3yr/$40M. While Ty Okada started 11 games in 2025 and is an internal answer, and Nick Emmanwori showed versatility as a rookie, the external additions (Rodney Thomas II on 1yr/minimum, D'Anthony Bell on 1yr/minimum) are special-teams depth — not starter investments. Safety has escalated from a depth concern to a real need. The Seahawks should look for a starting-caliber safety in rounds 2-4 of the draft.

Josh Jobe retained on 3yr/$24M at excellent value — starting CB2 locked up through 2028. Devon Witherspoon remains on rookie deal as the CB1. However, Riq Woolen's departure (PHI, 1yr/$15M) leaves a hole at CB3. Igbinoghene and Jean-Charles are minimum-deal flyers, not a reliable third corner. CB need drops from tier 2 to tier 3 — no longer urgent, but still worth addressing in mid-rounds of draft.
Drake Thomas retained on 2yr/$8M (outstanding value), joining Ernest Jones IV and Tyrice Knight. All three LBs are under multi-year deals. LB is essentially off the draft board as a need.

Rashid Shaheed re-signed on 3yr/$51M ($34.7M guaranteed). Starting WR corps of JSN (contract extension coming), Kupp ($13.5M in 2026), Shaheed, and Tory Horton is excellent. Dareke Young's departure to LV is minimal. WR is not a draft need.

Charles Cross and Abraham Lucas return as starters. Josh Jones re-signed on 1yr deal as swing tackle. Full five-starter continuity (Cross, Lucas, Zabel, Bradford, Sundell) is rare and valuable. OT is not a draft need.

Sam Darnold is the franchise QB on a 3yr/$100.5M deal. Drew Lock returns as the backup (year 2 of 2yr/$5M deal). QB is a non-need, though Seattle has done 30 visits with draft QBs suggesting long-term developmental interest.

key_additions.headline: Pro Bowl returner and playmaker re-signed; only player in NFL with both kick and punt return TDs in 2025
Starting CB locked up at elite value — 4th-lowest completion rate allowed in NFL (47.7%) in 2025
First-year starter and potential Pro Bowler retained at steal-of-offseason value after 96 tackles, 3.5 sacks
Bridge RB addition after Walker departure; 4.5 YPC career average and 226-lb frame fits run-first scheme
Former two-year Colts starter adds safety competition and special teams depth to replace Coby Bryant
Former first-round pick adds CB depth behind Witherspoon and Jobe after Woolen's departure
Super Bowl-winning swing tackle returns; started final 3 regular season games filling in for injured Charles Cross
Special teams captain re-signed; led all NFL players with 394 ST snaps in 2025
Rotational nose tackle returns to anchor interior depth behind Leonard Williams
Special teams standout re-signed; blocked punt vs. New Orleans and finished 4th on team in ST snaps

key_losses.headline: Super Bowl LX MVP departs as highest-paid RB ever; leaves massive hole with Charbonnet on ACL timeline
4-year Seahawk with 20 career sacks departs for AFC, thinning an EDGE rotation already aging at the top
Starting safety with 7 INTs over past two seasons leaves; Ty Okada expected to inherit starting role
Former Pro Bowler exits on prove-it deal; softer market than expected signals he had lost starting role to Jobe
Special teams/depth receiver follows OC Klint Kubiak to Las Vegas; minimal impact on WR depth

notes: Drew Lock (signed April 2025), Eric Saubert (signed March 2025), and River Cracraft (signed March 2025) were 2025 free agency signings already on the 2026 roster — not new 2026 FA transactions. They are included for roster completeness context but their contracts were signed in the prior league year. Rodney Thomas II and Josh Jones contract values represent best available data from Spotrac and OTC — Thomas terms remain partially undisclosed. Shemar Jean-Charles and Noah Igbinoghene contract values estimated at near-minimum based on PFR reporting that they will not impact the compensatory pick formula. Brady Russell 2-year deal terms not yet disclosed. A.J. Finley terms undisclosed.
